Name: 11_nlp_natural language_text_natural
Representative_Docs: ['it s a complicated task to achieve and comes under the umbrella of machine learning this field of work is known as natural language processing nlp the simplest approach would be to take a huge sample of text lets say reddit comments and then examine how often a word follows another word for example out of comments we might find that the word be comes after the word would times but the word be comes after the word alabama only time you colate all this data so that for each word in the comments you now have a list of every following word and how many times it was the following word if you sort these lists then you can work out the most common word to follow any other word from here you just pick a starting word and then probablistically select the next word based on your common follow words list i m simplifying the math a little here and that s about it for this method it won t produce neat sentances and sometimes the grammer and structure will be off the mark but it will produce semi readable text there are then numerous other approaches of different complexity which start to consider things like what type of words occur nouns verbs etc and under what rules should they be rejoined what sentiment is conveyed by a group of words and how should this be maintained openai also did some nlp work using a gigantic neural network which if you can get your head around some of the math could provide an interesting read', 'gt is it possible for a program to classify the bits of speech above as john talking and jane talking yes though the complexity of the structure itself can increase the complexity of the parser gt what do you call what i m trying to do i would say this would fall into parsing text in the natural language processing discipline gt are there any projects out there that attempt this any programming language even closed source i mostly just want to know if it s possible at all there is an entire field called natural language processing that does stuff like this though many in that field have moved onto writing algorithms that are attempting to extract the meaning of a sentence rather than just assign who is speaking it gt is this possible without ai it seems like there are rules to this but there are a ton of different scenarios err no so ai development is a discipline of programming where you write algorithms that will respond and react accordingly based on some given inputs machine learning is a type of ai development but there are static ai algorithms a good example is mini max it s an ai but it s not an ai that is ever going to approach natural intelligence the most basic ai s are chains of if statements since this will require some amount of conditional statements and reacting to inputs then your algorithm that parses text to assign who the speaker is will be an ai by some definition now if you skew the ai definition to being exclusively machine learning or as some have done deep learning then no don t necessarily need to use deep learning or machine learning to solve this problem but that doesn t mean the non ml solution will be trivial', 'gt i am not convinced that natural language at least as used by actual humans would be computationally expensive to parse the amount of text understood at once is fairly small after all the problem is that natural language as a deterministic language is known to be at least recursively enumerable notably because at a certain points one must enumerate every possible parse because of the ambiguity modern natural language parsers of course use statistical models with assistance from machine learning but that means that of the time the computer will misunderstand you completely and think it understood you perfectly let alone the more Representative_Docs: ['i am a software engineer working in data science and i will try to provide a few links later when i m not on my phone first things first though it sounds like you are starting tons of extra time take a stats or math double major that would be the best way to improve your resume with coursework majors actually mean something if your institution is accredited and so are more reliable secondly even a double major isn t all that impressive if we see a resume with a double major and minors and nothing else we will toss that resume we are looking for relevant internships any software internships contributions to big projects with other people extracurricular projects any extracurricular work in that order a resume with an internship is far stronger than a resume with a double major a resume with some outside project that you can talk about intelligently is far stronger than a resume with a double major coursework does not show qualification there are tons of people Name: 16_ar_vr_ar vr_ai
Representative_Docs: ['vr ar is cool from the typical gamer s perspective but still very niche a lot of people didnt have the gaming rigs required to power the og vive and rift but there are a lot more vr options on the market now that need less computing power to operate i see businesses trying to utilize this new tech but its still very niche and not going to be a core part of many projects i think a problem i see regarding ar vr is that it requires so much knowledge with very little payoff right now you need to know how the hardware works how to calculate the graphics as well as the human psychology of how a user experiences its a lot to know and not a lot of opportunities out there when compared to something like web design which is pretty easy to pick up personally i think the tech will grow and evolve to a point where it it will be ubiquitous in the corporate world as well as the creative and entertainment worlds but a lot of work has to be into the field before that happens in regards to its importance to ai as much as i love the idea of immersing one self in a virtual augmented world advanced ai will undoubtedly hold a much greater presence in our future some might think using vr ar allows us see problems with a different perspective but at the end of the day its still up to the user to solve those problems advanced ai will be able to solve those problems for us with minimal input from the user maybe vr allows me to see a d perspective of a brain tumor but advanced ai will be able to find the cure for all cancers although if you are truly passionate and determined you can certainly combine the two fields if you get creative just ask yourself what you are really interested in a large pay check perhaps making cool technologies or saving lives you are essentially asking what are the tools that will be important in the future but you should be asking what are the problems that you personally think we should be solving the rest will follow', 'no i m afraid i m not sure it will i spent two years working at an ar vr startup writing webgl mobile apps presently they are laying off almost all their d staff the problem is that whilst ar and vr are exciting technologies on the cusp of feasibility there still aren t that many commercial applications outside gaming we were doing real estate vr tours which sounds like a neat idea but we just couldn t get users to actually bother using the hardware a simple property brochure website in person visit was fine for them so it has gone with a lot of vr startups who if i m frank have been solutions just looking for a problem we did have some computer vision people but it s a very narrow highly specialised field all our vision research engineers were holders of phd or postdoc degrees and even then most of their work seemed to be porting white paper algorithms into java outside of vr and ar is gaming personally i would avoid this field for reasons you must have heard many times before as for business webapps writing crud apps isn t very glamorous neither is a lot of d work at the heart of it even in gaming you are more likely to be writing uis and creative tooling than anything powering a graphics engine which tends to be a commodity technology these days anyhow business apps also have some interesting but less obvious challenges when it comes to domain modelling distributed systems security and occasionally ui visualisation i used to do a lot of web d and d animation which can be quite demanding if you re targeting mobile some of those phones they use in the developing world have very tight resource constraints an important point it s great you re finding university stimulating and it s good to let your academic passions guide your career interests but you must remember that most jobs are not nearly as interesting or challenging as university you might have the odd hard problem but of the work is pretty bread and butter look at me i studied english literature i wrote about the history of ideas and the development of domesticity through the lens of th century drama i got to spend my days thinking about how the burgeoning th century middle class stimulated new literary formats in western europe i had a term where i got to learn about the history of writing systems fascinating stuff now my job is mostly about taking strings parsing them and shoving them in a database somewhere sometimes i get to do something requiring some curious algo or other eg gesture detection or very performance sensitive the d mobile web stuff set top box client dev or maybe some meaty distributed systems problem but by and large most of my work is fairly unremarkable that s okay because doing that gives me the skills and the opportunity to do the other but it s still a compromise i have to make as the alternative is doing a phd and then earning poverty wages as an adjunct lecturer in literary criticism so in a nutshell by all means follow a passion for d but make sure your expectations are realistic and don t discount other options prematurely most jobs are fairly boring but each comes with occasionally interesting work and your choice of specialism is really about choosing the flavour of that', 'gt no offense but are you sure about this fairly sure but i ve been wrong before gt i don t want to sound in denial i m not i m genuinely trying to learn but a lot of different trends in computer science seem to indicate to me that graphics vision programming will become big in years including alright let s examine them gt rapid advancements in gpus rather than cpus well sure but gpus becoming faster and cheaper does not stimulate demand for graphics software likewise demand for graphics software does not translate into loads of jobs writing low level d code to use a historical analogy over the last eighteen years the number of network connected devices in the world has increased many times over but a sizeable proportion of developers do not spend their days implementing tcp ip stacks network software is commodity software and i suspect a lot of ar vr tech will be similar gt rapid advances in ai which definitely fuels research in computer vision which is at the core of ar vr questionable imo and still doesn t turn into jobs writing either loads of d code or computer vision algorithms both will be commoditised into libraries or even third party services why would i pay a graduate to write their own graphics code when i could pay them to import a library written by a phd and then use the rest of their time scrubbing munging different data sources also you seem to be conflating computer vision and d work which is understandable but i think misguided they have overlapping uses in things like ar but are ultimately very different fields gt major tech giants all dumping money into vision and ar vr there are lots of reasons tech companies make big investments in exciting new technologies they re making a bet they have lots of capital that is better spent on moonshot gambles than sitting in a bank they are scared a competitor will innovate in the space and find some way to steal their lunch investors really like it none of which say much about the growth of ar any more than the late s heavy investment in wearables turned into anything beyond sports watches gt am i really dumb for thinking we re on the cusp of an ar vr revolution no not at all but it might be prudent not to stake your whole career on it if i had to bet i d say the main uses at least for the next few years will be advertising and events i know of a few digital agencies hiring webgl people to do things like product launch websites retail experiences etc look at agencies like eventstag or koffeecup in the uk most of their projects are things like an ar app to imagine owning a jaguar a shell oil interactive demonstrating co emissions during logistics maybe that s the sort of work you re interested in digital agencies don t pay that much though look you seem to be really really interested in ar and vr so much so i think it could be a good choice whatever the industry conditions you don t need there to be hundreds of vr jobs you just need there to be one that fits your skills i m just not convinced after working in the ar vr space myself for two years that either software or hardware are the things preventing user uptake it might remain a niche technology but if it s a real personal interest i think you can overcome that by showing employers your passion and expertise']

Representative_Docs: ['so i realize that making ai and stuff is far from a begginers task but i have no idea where to start i have basic knowledge on using python but that s kinda it things like that starting point specifically you should look into a there s a neat website that has a visual display of how it works compared to other algorithms if you pop js a pathfinding into google it should show up around the top for collision i suppose that depends on how deep you want to go do you want to learn how to do the calculations your self or do you want to learn how to use collision libraries learning it yourself will ultimately make you a better developer in the long run but it s no easy task i would start out by implementing a vector math library if you want to learn about collision after doing that i would figure out how to tell if a point is inside a triangle read sat amp x b for animation transitions i don t really know what you mean under what context unity your own custom solution some library it s hard to know if you re talking about a simple transition between values or interfacing with some pre built system if you re talking about rolling your own solution then it depends on the goals you aim to accomplish the same goes for particle systems are you looking to implement your own you might start by optimizing for having many objects at once using some sort of object pooling system then you should figure out what you want your particles to do and research how to accomplish those goals personally i learned these things by creating my own game engine after i had been programming for a few months it was no easy task but it taught me a lot of what i know today about mathematical concepts in game research is key and spending time deciding what it is exactly that you want with persistence i m sure you ll end up figuring things out best of luck', 'the fields of ai and ml are huge so i will just focus on ml as that is the area i m most experienced in there are many approaches and sub fields within ml and you will find that most ml models algorithms fall within three categories supervised learning used to classify items into predefined categories requires labelled training data showing correct classification for some example data a good starting point would be linear regression some more complex but commonly used algorithms include neural networks and support vector machines unsupervised learning used to group items together based on similarities often called clustering requires a training set of data but does not required labels a good starting point would be k means or k nearest neighbour reinforcement learning used to select actions based on the state of the environment and a reward given these models are often used to control robotics or games without requiring any training data the models learn by doing the best start here would be to look into q learning i would recommend picking a specific ml approach and learning that to get started there is a great video series on an introduction to neural networks by sebastian lague which would be a good starting point just an advanced warning though the field of ml is basically applied statistics so you will need a pretty good math background to follow along with the process in particular you will need to be happy with linear algebra vectors matracies and transformations statistics probability and distributions and some calculus basic differentation and integration should be enough best of luck']

Name: 19_deep_deep learning_algorithm_learning
Representative_Docs: ['but what is this stuff imagine you want to buy a flat and get a fair price for it so you scout your local auction sites and eventually build a database of flats each has the following information size in square meters price in if you plot this information you will end up with something like this each x represents one data point you will see some kind of pattern in here it s not perfect by any means but you can see that the larger the house the more expensive it is now do you think you could use this information to estimate how much should you pay for a specific flat well if you have a decent math background then you might have heard of least squares method that s pretty much what we want to apply there without going into too much detail let s do this we have drawn a line over these points it doesn t pass through any directly but it s a fairly good estimator eg if you had a house of square meters you could put that into the equation assuming you calculated a and b and it woudl spit out the price for you like this now in practice you will likely use more variables than just size of the house there might be age of the house distance from supermarkets number of bathrooms and bedrooms and how good is neighbourhood but the gist stays the same so what does machine learning in this case it s linear regression do is given all these datapoints is calculate you a and b letting you extrapolate results you have obtained to determine brand new outcomes eg even if you have never seen a square meters house in your dataset you could easily plug it into this equation that s pretty much what machine learning is at it s core of course in practice often it won t often be a linear regression task with infinite possible results but say determine whether a given picture is of a cat in which case input data is x pixels matrix whether mail is spam or ham so your input would likely be a list of words in the email which you would then compare to existing ones that you know are spam etc okay is that why i read something like it was easier to do the better gpu you had yes and no training a machine learning algorithm is done in step it will start with drawing a pretty much random line then you start feeding it your testing examples and telling it how off it is eg if you predicted a price of and it was then you are off so your algorithm might change value of a parameter a tiny bit so now it s off then it will go over every single example and eventually reach a pretty good equation now this training is effectively matrix multiplication and it just turns out that gpus are pretty damn good at multithreaded matrix multiplications although it kinda depends on the type of algorithm some actually do run better on your standard cpu eg if you need very high precision', 'deep learning is a subset of machine learning that uses neural networks with multiple hidden layers hence the part deep learning that s where this depth comes from so there are no differences every deep learning algorithm is a machine learning algorithm but not every machine learning algorithm will be classified as deep learning', 'in order for something to be considered deep learning you need to have multiple hidden layers so for instance linear regression or naive bayes despite both being machine learning do not fall under deep learning neural networks generally are considered deep learning with the exception of perceptrons as these effectively have just one layer so it s not about the problem you encounter but about the solution you employ recommender system doesn t necessarily need to utilize any deep learning algorithm in fact the one employed in the course which i heavily recommend utilizes collaborative filtering algorithm which while definitely fits into unsupervised machine learning algorithm cannot be classified as a deep learning solution but there are ways of using deep learning when making such a recommender system the difference is essentially in whether you directly map set of inputs to an output f x y or whether it s x f x g f x h g f x y technically you can tackle any problem that traditionally uses deep learning with a standard non neural network based algorithm the only question is how effective will it be eg neural networks and deep learning are very good at working with images as they can create intermediate functions like edge detection blurring focusing on a specific color look for specific shapes etc rather than directly try and decide whether a pile of pixels is a cat this will be hard to accomplish with say basic logistic regression']

Name: 20_image_images_opencv_vision
Representative_Docs: ['gt with regards to datasets does that mean i d need to download and somehow implement a dataset of thousands of faces into my application wouldn t that be a pretty huge undertaking even from a storage and optimization standpoint not really to begin with each individual image is most likely going to be small say x pixels that gives you pixels a png of that size is going to take idk kilobytes multiply that by and you end up with a total of megabytes hardly a problem nowadays even if you used much larger images say a whole megabyte per image that s gb this might sound like much and it is but you will only need this for the training phase after finishing training your neural network it will be around mb effectively you are using this many images so it learns what makes up for a face so your network will first figure out how to detect edges on a picture then it will probably learn basic shapes then it will look for combinations of said shapes eg rectangles hand i am simplifying a bit but effectively neural networks work in layers and each next layer uses what previous one has determined as an input to get towards the result which is a is it a face or a hand or something else this pretty much happens automatically you as a programmer just have to figure out a general model to use and give it a sufficient dataset this might sound a bit magical so here s a much simpler example linear regression say that you want to figure out price of a house in a given city so you find offers and plot them like this x depicts a single data point you have found for now you only have one variable size of the house you can clearly see a trend in this data while a bit noisy you can tell that the bigger the house the more it will cost what machine learning will try to accomplish is find a function that fits this data so effectively f size price like so this function won t be perfect obviously as you can see but it s a fairly good fit for the data and it s error rate is actually not bad as in sum of these green lines will be relatively small especially compared to something like this which is where your machine learning algorithm might very well start so for data points you will only end up with a single equation it might be linear can be quadratic can be polynomial etc which takes like bytes in memory if all you have is a single datapoint then obviously there can be an infinite number of lines that pass through it and it s a perfect match make it data points however and your algorithm can figure out a fairly good match for the data as a single datapoint will now only make up for of it s overall decision of course images are a lot larger rather than a single size variable you would have aforementioned but that s really the gist of it program doesn t try to learn every single face or hand it tries to understand how features you give it pixels affect the decision whether something is a face or a hand using this data hence resulting model is nowhere near as large as data you used to train it and it works much faster eg training might take an hour whereas a finished model can figure it out in miliseconds gt so hopefully jumping into a python based application might not be as daunting as it first looks oh you could write a machine learning algorithm in c if you prefer that p in fact python frameworks are written in c most of the time it s going to be annoying however what in python will take you literally lines of code can be in c i can tell yuo that for instance with keras and python i just had to create directories called pedestrian car and bike and fill each with image resolution didnt matter neither did them being colored or b amp w or even png vs bmp and feed it to one lines of code function and it was enough to transform all into normalized size matrices to feed right into my learning algorithm gt all in all though this seems like considerably more of a complex undertaking than i originally thought it d be well i don t know how your country education system works but in mine your final thesis is generally a pages text on a specific subject accompanied by an application you effectively have nearly a year to finish it although in practice most are doable in months and it s the final step before you get your degree in terms of difficulty of those your project would fit right in it s neither disgustingly complex eg i have seen some that effectively said go build a d game engine from scratch but also not too simple', 'just as a warning this could be quite a complex problem you may have already assumed this as you are looking at machine learning approaches also depending on the number of images you have available to train your machine learning model you may not get super accurate results that being said there may be a slightly simpler approach check out this stackoverflow post and see how you may be able to achieve this without using machine learning this approach is basically detecting a change in gradient in your image based on some threshold then counting the number of independent gradient changes if all your images of cells are quite clear distinctly different from the background in the image and you only ever want to count the total number of cells then this will work for you it may take some tweaking to get your images pre processed correctly and set a good threshold if you have images with backgrounds which are varying uneven or if you have multiple types of cell in one image and you only want to count the number of a certain type of cell then you will probably need to use some machine learning model there are a few different approaches you could take here as you suggest in your inital post you could see this as a regression problem although it could also be seen as a classification problem if you solve this as a regression problem then you will able to identify the number of cells but you won t be able to determine the location in the image where the cells where located if you solve this using a detection method classification then you will also be able to identify where the cells are within the image one approach would be to use some type of support vector machine svm or a convolutional neural network cnn although there are plenty of other options which could also work you may be able to build a model without a deep understanding but it will be difficult and you may not fully grasp how the models are working if you really want to learn how this achieved then there is some pre requisite math required linear algebra vectors matracies and transformations statistics probability and distributions calculus some differentiation and integration you will also need a lot of sample images to train this model a few thousand images minimum for good results and ideally all these images will be labeled meaning the cells are pre identified by a human in some way gt also what does few features would be important under regression mean a feature refers to some characteristic which is being used to determine what an object is for example consider a flower some features would be petal width petal length petal count stem height stem diameter etc the cells in your image are likely to have a lot less features than a human face for example best of luck', 'gt i just need to match one original image with a set of x images that are the same product if that makes sense you just want something that really is more of an ongoing research than something you can implement i know that for you a human this looks simple but for a computer this is what it sees upper left corner of both pictures rgb data and on the second one given this kind of data representation you tell me how they are similar to each other as this is what your computer is actually seeing color intensity at a given location it doesn t know anything beyond that and there s nothing similar between the two these colors are different backgrounds are different hues are different these things are also immense this is x px image this means individual pixels and each every single one of them affects the overall equation of hey this image kinda looks like the other one in order to determine whether two pictures show the same thing you first need to get to the representation of that thing on a much higher level than a mere pixel map right now it s noise you have to remove said noise starting from backgrounds and move from operating on pixels onto operating on groups of pixels that together make up a particular feature eg a curve a specific shape etc and from then onto bundling these features together into specific geometric parameters task you have set to do is actually a really one to do reminds me of this except your is harder since you want to do it without supervised algorithm you want an unsupervised clustering that can group multiple objects similar to each other in some way together this sounds unfriendly but let s consider what s the alternative that does not use machine learning to help out first i would look into extracting features from this image look into algorithms used to remove backgrounds and in particular into image gradients and derivatives once you have that you might end up with something that looks like this more specifically this is an image that was treated with a convolution matrix then you binarize the input leaving just colors black and white like this then you start looking at white objects and shapes they make might consider vectorizing them using lagrange polynomial or something of that sort you will need to read some papers on how it s actually done nowadays rather than using raster data then given each white object you compare it after rotating it by say degrees in each direction step by step to objects that you have detected in similar fashion to ones on other picture if similarity is sufficient eg you can find something resembling rain drops on both then you consider these identical catch oh boy a lot of them first shape analysis is not a simple thing second you need an algorithm that can ignore the scale eg you want something that won t be bothered by the fact that curves and angles are identical but one is bigger than the other cuz camera was closer and yes you will end up using a whole set of linear algebra and non linear matrix transformations p you probably will want to look into opencv library has bindings for most of popular languages it s good at this sort of thing so if you were to ask me to do this i would probably consider a hybrid approach use a convolutional neural network to classify on whether or not image has some specific attributes say a person a shirt a belt a claw mark a hoodie and then use that as a source for my comparison between two pictures it wouldn t classify two images as identical or similar itself it would just extract multiple preset features that i could then use for comparison catch is that you would need a substantial dataset say labeled pictures for starters and then you train a set of neural networks for each feature on a picture']

Name: 21_ee_engineering_ce_computer
Representative_Docs: ['as someone with a cs degree as well as an electrical engg degree and a minor in computer engg which were both in the same department as software engineering who was friends with a lot of students in software engineering i d suggest you do software engineering here s why first off there are going to be a lot of overlap courses things like basic programming classes first year along with data structures classes a database course and a networking course are all pretty standard in both that you can t change from the math perspective you ll probably do a discrete math course boolean algebra graph theory etc which is pretty important for understanding data structures and algorithms and at least one calculus course besides that the two start to diverge quite a bit in general software engineering courses in your third and fourth year are fairly project management software testing and program design heavy this will cover a lot of stuff related to designing large scale software projects an easy way to think of this is to look at microsoft office and think i wonder how they got hundreds of people to make a unified project that was efficient worked well with the other programs e g copy pasting from excel to word automatically makes a table in word and tested the entire thing answers to that are what are covered in your software engineering classes on the other hand computer science in your third and forth years will be much more theory heavy all cs majors will usually go through some fairly tough algorithms courses which get into very technical algorithm design correctness and optimality this can be fairly math heavy and while it certainly isn t high level math there is a lot of it other courses you might take would be in parallel systems parallel algorithms computer and network security artificial intelligence big data advanced data bases compiler and language design functional programming a math heavy programming paradigm used by languages like haskell scala and closure and quantum computing i hope i don t need to point out the depth of the math for this class many of those examples i listed will have a decent amount of math again not high level but lots of it as professors in computer science tend to prefer lecturing about the subject from a theoretical and mathematical perspective as a simple analogy the differences are something similar to civil engineering software engineering and material science computer science while both subjects have lots of overlap they both need to know about the properties of steel and stuff like that material science dives into the fundamentals of creating the best brick for every situation algorithm network design while the civil engineers don t care about how a brick is made but they want of them and need to make sure the entire structure doesn t collapse and everyone needs to know how to work together to put all the bricks together assuming you don t care to go into a grad school program or work in something like data science machine learning artificial intelligence you re almost certainly better off in software engineering as you will be using a lot of the things that you use daily in a job where as in computer science you often go into more theory based classes than project based classes one quick thing to note is that if software engineering requires you to take a common core year or two with all the other engineering majors you may have to take a number of math heavy courses that really don t apply to your degree like advanced calculus and physics i know at my school a common complaint was that the software engineering students had to take a bunch of unrelated courses because all engineers had to take those courses whereas the computer science students got a bit more flexibility in choosing their courses so they didn t waste time in classes they didn t ever need this is very university specific so make sure to compare the curriculums and ask a cs se student at that university they often are friends with students in the other major so they hear the complaints on both sides', 'before you decide on a degree search up a syllabus for both ee ce and cs to get an idea of what you ll learn at my school ee is all analog classes with a microcontrollers embedded and dsp making up the extent of software learned can get cs jobs and some ce jobs depending on what you know electives but typically you ll have to learn to code on your own ce has a solid mix of ee and cs classes which can lead you into embedded ai and cloud computing varies by school you won t go in depth for electronics power or rf though it ll focus heavily on digital design low level architecture can t get certain ee jobs with it like ones mentioned above unless you tailor your electives to one of those areas cs focuses on prepping you to work as a developer whether its full stack ai ml data security etc it is easier but you can t get ee ce jobs with it miss fundamental coursework as a disclaimer these are generalisations some schools have a cs program which involves ee classes or an ee program which involves lots of cs classes so do your research you ll make similar money with all degrees unless you work for a faang which pays more than some typical entry to mid career eng roles', 'i m a sophomore in college torn between majoring in ee or cs my end goal is to go to grad school and go into research not so much interested in industry jobs if anyone could hear me out and offer some insight i d greatly appreciate it i loved the cs classes i took the basics up to data structures and algorithms and am very interested in studying cs theory and discrete math further however i m worried about missing out on the interesting math that only ee majors learn specifically probability stochastic signal theory information theory signal processing and the like i know probability and other ee math is relevant for machine learning ai which is why ee math seems promising for a potential cs master s in this area but i m not sure if it s a good idea to gear my undergrad studies toward hot technological trends career wise even so information theory and signal processing are very interesting to me and the math seems more ee oriented my concern with going down the ee route is not getting to learn about compilers operating systems discrete math and theory of computation basically not learning how computers computing work a very strong interest of mine as well these classes are exclusive to cs majors at my school and i worry about missing out on the interesting mathy problems in cs the areas of cs that interest me the most are the theoretical mathematics side ai computer vision cryptography and coding theory not so much software development basically the math of cs i m guessing the benefits of ee in undergrad for something more cs oriented down the road i ve heard is that it is easier to shift from ee to cs but not the other way around and that understanding electronics on the lowest level will bridge my understanding between high level cs algorithms and what s going on under the hood i m not sure how true this is since the ee curriculum at my school doesn t deal with computer architecture much no matter what getting a strong theoretical and math background is important to me i m just torn between focusing more continuous ee math or discrete cs math should i consider a pure math major instead sadly applied math is not a degree option for me side note the compe major at my school is not as math intensive as i d like and mostly focused on embedded systems which isn t my main goal tldr i m wondering how to choose between ee and cs if i am in love with the theories and math behind both thanks']

Representative_Docs: ['waldorf teacher here it does matter if you are personally fulfilled and happy your self development should matter as much as the students any school that promotes self care self development and holistic health in their faculty will experience much higher teacher quality and thus student learning in waldorf education we focus on the mind spirit and the soul of both students and teachers involved waldorf education honors the bond between educators and pupils and recognizes that bringing joy into the classroom is so much more important in the long run than just checking boxes off state standards your personal growth development and happiness impact your students and the world beyond far more than you will ever know your work impacts eternity remember ai education will never inspire students will never offer advice will never give students role models or give students an outlet to go to with their problems robots will never be able to replace all the things we do as teachers that go beyond academics ai can t give students a shoulder to lean on a hug when needed or true encouragement when necessary it s equivalent to and sorry if this is a harsh comparison the difference between pornography and making love sure the computer can do something but true human interaction is another thing completely know that you bringing joy and laughter into the classroom is extremely important even if the administration is too shallow to see that your heartbeat is the pulse of learning and leading humanity towards a greater good keep shining bright keep being a light in a dark place', 'submission statement all links included in this article are for reference purposes only and are all in context with the views and subjects discussed on this thread hey all i decided to start this thread based upon much of my personal thoughts research aspirations conversations experiences and various understandings of education psychology education funding teaching learning through play learning through experience sensory learning skill mastery career choice career progression the skill gap technology ai machine learning it big data struggling communities learning difficulties troubled youth youth services youth gangs exclusion from education access to education qualifications accreditation student debt education standards and education policies be aware this is still a non exhaustive list of knowledge related subjects topics i will not be going too in depth with everything on this one post as i hope to and hope that other threads will be created and or sighted in relation to each part of the total subject matter here so what is the actual subject the subject of this thread is intended to revolve around the acquisition understanding use and value of knowledge itself when applied to an individual taking into consideration the study of collective consciousness learning skills employment history and future including it ai and ml starting from the top we will firstly be looking at how we acquire and in coordination process knowledge which will lead us to the subject of understanding before we can start to look at knowledge acquisition at all we must first look at when we begin to actually acquire knowledge and why as i said earlier i wont go too in depth but rather sight some studies connected with this particular area of research looking at the human learning curve experience it would probably likely widely agreed that we all begin learning at the moment we are born into this world and there are studies far and wide detailing research into this however there are also just as many and more often the same studies looking at our knowledge acquisition starting far before this within the last weeks of our mothers pregnancy while we developing in the womb the point here is to prove that we as humans and also most living beings consistently learn even with severe disablement as our first and only everlasting involuntary action to put it more into context i have an old but favourite riddle that i throw into conversations from time to time what is the only thing that we as humans always are doing even involuntarily as long as we are alive now when i ask this i am normally met with the common perception answer seeking suggestions of seeing breathing and living among many other good close but not as accurate actions interestingly the answer living is quite true as there are many debates and studies related to the definitions of the word taking the above into account i m sure that you too have heard the phrase you live and you learn before and whilst the phrase rings true it also could be said that you live as you learn or you learn as you live switching around the use of the words and as don t they all just amount to the same reasoning if you believe yes then wouldn t it be fair to say that living in the context of is actually learning given that you can agree with the above or the most part of it could we not agree that knowledge is the sum of learning even further to this whilst learning an individual will also gain wisdom from long term repetitive problem solving and also experience failure success but doubly both in sum of and by product of themselves and all of the above i may be going a little off topic here or am i once we ve acquired the above we process this learning into well skills actually if we look at the hour rule taking into consideration deliberate learning hours we actuality would display applied skills in whatever we have learned our bodies would have learned to adjust to what our brains have been processing just as with human evolution development the next question is how do we measure and then value our knowledge well it could be argued that knowledge is valued through the need of others for skills advice and or information the saying each one teach one ties into this heavily as for everything that one person learns they must teach as not only will they give but they will also gain knowledge in the process everything from a persons reactions to their responses will be new knowledge to the person who gave knowledge skill even if they knew how the person would react in actuality they only made at best an informed guess as to what the reaction would be only after the individual reacts can they learn something from the knowledge they have just given hopefully you re still with me here but my understanding is in essence knowledge and skills are valued by those who require it along with those who possess it currently employment and education economies are represented by a skewed valuation of these things as consensus on the valuations all depends on many factors but mostly regional location subject and the prior knowledge accessible i say this because different parts of the world and different initiatives projects etc need different amonut of skills knowledge or skilled people this along with a culmination of prior common and specific knowledge enables the learning needed for said skill and skill level justification this again is still given attribute on the basis of the accreditable standard within said region now if we look at that from what we would call a collective consciousness angle given we could all tap into that collective or shared resource purposefully and at will then we have a greater level of consensus globally for skill knowledge levels accreditation and finally value i d imagine this to be a kind of real time accreditation self valuation and value by trial all in one as you would be born and immediately able to draw from a vast library of history and information which humanly you would process by consensus i say this as the most probable answers to any question would be backed up by all knowledge that came before your perception i say perception instead of conception as your pre birth learning curve would have only been from the point of receiving stimulus and perceiving the identity frequency etc of that stimulus question for thought what if these consensuses could be actualised agrred on and shared in real time i ve been slowly typing this for a while as i wanted to get a lot out and have lots more theories and research to speak on also im not really one for the limelight however now that ive posted it i hope to invoke some healthy discussion debate and progressive thought actions if any of what i have written is really interesting to you as a study then please reply or message me as i am always interested in this conversation and wish to collaborate with others who have a similar train of thought i will be back with a continuation of thoughts soon thank you references life when do babies begin to learn while in womb babies begin learning language from their mothers language experienced in utero affects vowel perception after birth a two country study can dead brains be brought back to life first human study to find out learning learning curve collective consciousness morphic resonance knowledge definition', 'million that s how many teachers we re missing globally in education departments coffers around the world are also stretched in all but a few countries the uk is itself under huge financial stress with of schools reported to be in deficit in but put yourself in the education minister of tanzania s shoes she has twice the number of children as the uk but only of the budget it s worth noting that according to the latest projections by the majority of learners on earth will be in africa too often the jumping off point when discussing ai is the ponderance as to whether it is desirable to see jonny the terminator wall e teaching algebra in guilford this is the wrong place to start we must look at the global context we must start with the desperate unmet need for quality education across the developing world for it is there that change will happen first but for a second let s put aside whether robots in education are desirable and see if it s actually feasible ask a friend in any industry if a robot could ever do their job they will tell you very firmly no their job is too complex too varied there are parts that can never be done by a robot however advanced the ai here s the thing they re both right and wrong you see robots don t do jobs they do tasks ask that friend again ask them if there are any tasks in their job that could be potentially automated most will say yes and that s how automation takes over industries the manufacturing jobs that donald trump bemoans have gone to china in the most part haven t they were simply broken up into different tasks tasks that were easy to automate were automated tasks that were harder were reconstituted into new human jobs there were just fewer of them and they were only available to highly skilled people but teaching is very complex so many skills intertwined from empathy amp non verbal communication through to subject knowledge and more surely teaching is the exception that proves the rule it s certainly not the easiest set of tasks to automate but split these tasks into a series of smaller ones and even in when ai is barely out of it s digital nappies it can do almost every one of them in some cases better than a human can we stitch them together to create robo mr jenkins that can do all these tasks exactly as a human would no we re at least a decade off that probably more but that may actually be less important than one might think so what does this actually mean for our profession honestly i think it s really exciting we have the ability to redefine what it means to be a teacher in a way that works for both student and teacher for starters i see no reason why the teacher of the future does any marking or any admin but then come the more difficult decisions does it make sense that at any given moment we have a million teachers all teaching isosceles triangles would it be better for us to curate the best lessons and just work with those what might we lose by doing that what might we gain might teachers move much more towards being learning guides and mentors rather than custodians of knowledge does that empower or detract does that create more jobs or less all this is up for grabs all yet to be defined one thing we do know is that our industry will change it s inevitable it s now up to us to ensure we preserve what s great about teaching yet not be afraid to rethink key aspects we need to move fast though over a billion children depend on us making the right choices']

Name: 24_company_team_boss_work
Representative_Docs: ['long post ahead but i am feeling hopeless and useless for many an offer at a major swe company faang as a fresh grad would be a golden ticket but for me it has proven to be a major thorn that has repeatedly stab me in the feelings a few months back i got an offer for a new grad swe position at the big blue social media company yes the company full of scandals and privacy issues which is important in this discussion of course you would wanna tell your cs friends about your fortune windfall after all who doesn t so i told them that i got an offer at that company big mistake my friends instead of being congratulatory at the very least have some tact like not commenting if you have nothing nice to say started to mock me some of the things they said to me include you are going to be a code monkey doing useless crud php apps every day of course they would hire you sarcasm intended you are joining a company that stores password in plaintext it shows your talent i wouldn t work for that company it s evil context that is the first thing they said after i told them i got an offer it would be okay if i asked for an opinion i kinda felt sad and hurt but i took it in a positive light thinking that they are just doing some sort of a roast me i mean real friends mock each other right i m not the bottom of the barrel cs student so those comments don t reflect my abilities however they kept going at it sending me every single bad article about the company together with commentary and i said enough is enough obviously i told them that i am fed up with this mocking and at least give me some room to breathe no they aren t having it and they claim that it shouldn t hurt your ego or straight up asking me to man up funny to think that rambling here might not be the best way to man up of course the best course of action is to play with it and find some fodder to roast them back one of my friends who roasted me just got a phd offer and i took the idea of roasting that person talking about how a phd would basically be years of slavery friend s response gt at least i m doing machine learning research that will have tremendous impact and not become a stupid php coding monkey for some lizard ceo oh i m getting paid quite good while doing so so i m not going to be a poor phd student i soon realized that my friend is doing what we call an industrial phd which means that they are getting paid market rate to do a phd there s not much i can roast back at them and i backed down my other roasting friends are doing phds and getting paid handsomely going to hft top tier companies and some are doing masters in top rated universities for cs this combined with my code monkey offer means that i am at the bottom of the food chain amongst my friends and am their punching bag i went and applied like crazy to many top end hfts and other companies the result i got rejected by all of them in varying stages that s fine we all get rejected right well i didn t tell the roasters about my pursuit but i did tell someone i could confide with and he was very encouraging about me trying to achieve better i don t know what happened but word spread and they came back to me with another barrage of disparaging comments you re destined to be a php code monkey face it other companies do not want a person who break their codebase in production if you re so good why did other companies reject you this proves something this is the current state of affairs now an offer with the failing social media company with a culture of bad coding practices and collapsing management at least that is what the media spins it up to be i feel like throwing away the offer and joining an average joe coding shop i mean what s the difference between big coding change in the career path but let s start from the beginning as a child i was fond of pc games like so many others but it wasn t until years at the university getting general engineering degree that i rediscovered my interest in cs my friend and i were making an online game just for the fun of it then got into mcu programming which then led to taking several online courses on coursera this allowed me to find a job in a state research center it was really interesting and i had an opportunity to work with opengl and cuda we made programs for scientific calculations so i felt like i was doing something useful i had to invent algorithms and use my math knowledge as well which felt really fulfilling but i had to leave this job because of very low salary like really low and necessity to take secrecy access that would ban me from leaving the country after that i was involved into a start up with a friend up until i found a real job in cctv field i was expecting some image processing tasks and using of computer vision algorithms nut it turned out to be fixing casual bugs in legacy and doing some web development in the meantime i kept learning about different fields of cs from the most fundamental ones to some of the more specific in particular at the beginning i was vey interested in ml especially after watching andrew ng stanford course on youtube however practice made me realize it s not about cool math and real science nowadays but mostly about fighting with datasets and python frameworks which resulted in deep python dislike one of the courses that i ve taken golang webdev got me a job at one of the main russian it corporations and as i stated at the beginning it is basically backend web development i don t really like web development but i think highload problems can be fun especially optimizations and i really love golang i spent last half year to try to learn some hardcore math topology category theory etc not great results because it required much more time than i had but i really enjoyed it nonetheless learned functional programming with haskell just loved it i tried to learn computer graphics as well watched uc davis academics course introduction to computer graphics tried messing up with opengl ended up with a lot of textured crates flying camera and basic lighting tried to learn vulkan api as well and then basically i ve stuck it feels like interesting stuff to do but every time i read a tutorial or a forum i feel like a total noob that just doesn t get some basics and can t do anything i also helped my wife with image proccessing project and it was pretty fun reading the white paper understanding it s math and implementing it to work was just awesome but again python and numpy hate them we are also moving to canada next year but i don t want to continue the current career path and i think in a way it is a great timing to change it maybe try game development but to do graphics in aaa titels you should be experienced professional but in order to become such you ve got to be a mobile games developer for years or be doing some other routine which does not excite me at all haskell might also be an option but it seems Name: 27_healthcare_medicine_medical_clinical
Representative_Docs: ['gt model health disease data i ve looked into something similar and the reality is that the way computational statistical medicine is going is all about genomic proteomic sequencing i e omics in other words it s less about doctor s notes and more about taking some sample from the patient and running it through computational statistical analysis of cellular and molecular biological data to try to figure out what kind of diagnosis or treatment you want to give that s what precision medicine is basically as a math stats guy who has always had an interest in healthcare and medicine i ve spent some time looking into this and it seems like if i want to contribute in any meaningful way knowing how to analyze molecular biological data seems nearly unavoidable i would maybe take a bioinformatics class i think this field actually may use some nlp methods because when you sequence genomes you see a sequence of letters from the rna dna so sequences that look like gacttaactgatcca and you have to find mutations or patterns edit found a better article on this topic specifically for nlp survey of natural language processing techniques in bioinformatics edit here s just one example of an actual job using nlp to cancer data nlp data scientist amp data engineer at dana farber', 'gt my ideal career would be one where i get to bridge medicine and tech either in academia as a lead investigator researcher or in industry i look at some of the work that google ai s healthcare group does and can see myself working on similar problems for the rest of my life in that case check linkedin etc to see what kind of degrees the pis at google ai healthcare have my cents from the cs ai side imo an md cs phd sounds like a massive waste of time and energy if you really want to work on ai applied to healthcare get a job at google ai healthcare or someplace similar as a swe or if there s an academic lab doing ai healthcare research you re super excited about get a phd there then join industry as a researcher if you want to be a doctor get an md you don t need to load up on degrees in order to tackle interesting problems take anne wojcicki for example got a bio bs did some postbac bio research worked in industry then co founded andme w another bio bs if you really want an md phd for the title that s fine too but make sure your motivation to get the degree is clear before devoting the majority of your s it', 'i invest quite a lot in ai and in particular its application in health so i follow the space quite regularly i can tell you currently ai is not replacing doctors and likely won t anytime soon ai is going to have a very big impact on healthcare over this next decade however and a lot of stakeholders are very aware of this it will predominantly be used as tooling for clinicians the administrative side and patients to use as well as in research etc you have a lot of public health systems that are under funded and under staffed to meet the needs of the public in particular emergency departments can often be facing quite big bottlenecks in patient processing this is in part because a lot of patients simply don t now what constitutes emergency attention and what does not which they often try to address through education and awareness but honestly that s not going to solve it since if it was that easy they d already be doing it this is something where ai can help address this issue by providing both cost effective a lot of ai solutions are being built around inexpensive hardware for instance cameras microphones etc and scalable solutions so you already have these governing bodies looking into ai to see how it can help solve these issues for them in years time it will likely be a standard process for triage nurses to utilise some ai diagnostic tooling assuming the patient is present a common set of symptoms that one of their tools can be used for when prioritising patient inflow some diseases are under diagnosed due to difficulties scaling the current diagnostic approach either because it s very expensive or it s a cumbersome process one of these is sleep apnea for which many people unfortunately only find out they have it after it has caused them some other complication strike heart problems etc for which there is already work being done on providing ai solutions for this one of which has already achieved regulatory approval though only as a pre screening tool another has yet to submit but is likely to try get approved for diagnosis and another has done some early stage research there are emerging markets within healthcare which need new forms of diagnostic tooling and monitoring to really see that space grow and be adopted for instance telehealth has been growing year after year and is only going to keep getting bigger but the biggest problem it has is it obviously lacks the equipment and direct oversight that a doctor could give directly in their office currently there has been solutions for this in the form of developing hardware attachments for people s phones so data can be sent back to the doctor but this space will start utilising ai diagnostic solutions too especially solutions that are able to integrate right out of the box e g utilise what s already available on their phone or watch or pc disease management is another area that will benefit while there are already lots of monitoring and other solutions out there ai will take these to the next level to provide smarter methods for monitoring ongoing disease management now as far as how smart is ai well not very but are we on the cusp of real general intelligence probably not if we take the theory that seems the most likely way to achieve a general intelligence which is getting the computing power necessary to simulate the entire brain then we ve got a ways to go before such a thing would be possible however there s certainly the possibility that we find some other way of replicating intelligence regardless the thing is we don t really need ai to be truly smart or actually intelligent to be able to apply it to all of these different areas']

Name: 30_things_learn_just_anki
Representative_Docs: ['prioritization and making conscious decisions is something that you make a habit of it doesn t come easily and it requires constant practice and discipline we are always prioritizing things and deciding what to do next but those decisions are often made unconsciously our minds are lazy and will happily take shortcuts and make snap decisions those shortcuts often lead us towards short sighted behaviours consciously i want to lose weight but unconsciously i really want to enjoy that piece of cake consciously i want to work on a project but unconsciously it would be a lot more pleasurable to play games or read reddit left to our own devices we ll often take actions that are pleasurable to immediate primal needs but run counter to our conscious intentions we have to go out of our way and expend mental effort to make conscious decisions if you want to read more about it check out thinking fast and slow by daniel kahneman one way to avoid slipping into this autonomous way of thinking is to setup a routine as i mentioned before make a plan start the day by going over what you could be doing and what s most important at the end of the day look back on what you did and see if you followed your plan and if not why not sometimes you have good reasons to change plans but sometimes you lose your way and go off in another direction set aside a time for this write it down on the calendar set an alarm get up minutes early specifically to think about your plan whatever it takes it will be difficult at first and might be frustrating as you find yourself continually breaking from your plan but with time you ll find that you become more conscious of your plan it has become second nature for me to be in the middle of some distraction and think wait is this part of the plan should i go back to my plan or do i need to update my plan it helps to be skeptical too if you find yourself thinking maybe i should do something else instead you want to think no i need a darn good reason to change the plan is this really a good reason at the same time you don t want to get bogged down in planning some people lose entire days frozen with anxiety worrying they aren t spending their time properly only to find they have wasted their time just in thinking about it which reinforces their anxiety about misusing their time it can be a vicious cycle i don t think it s a big deal to most people but those with anxiety fight it every day being conscious of your decisions is only one part of it another part is judging priorities based on the right metrics again our unconscious mind is happy to take shortcuts choose things that are easy or interesting or quick or on the top of our mind these are rarely the most productive ways to achieve your goals having a clear goal in mind helps a lot whatever your task whatever your project ask yourself what s the purpose of this what do i hope to accomplish most importantly what does a solution look like once you have a clear idea of what you are trying to accomplish and what it looks like to accomplish it then you can judge every possible idea and task against how well it helps you accomplish that it s important that your goals have a clear end state and a clear time frame without a time frame you can justify any distraction i can get back to that later first i m going to do this thing that seems like more fun i ll get it done eventually without a clear end state you can justify any amount of scope creep you know it would be cool if it did this too that sounds like fun let s do that instead with a particular end state in mind and a particular time frame in which to accomplish it you can t do everything you have to prioritize which of these things is essential to my goal and which are just nice to have if i can t get it all done in time which pieces get me the closest to the goal what could i do without hint don t do without sleep it s essential to fulfilling your goals this is probably best explained through an example let s say you want to learn to program what does that mean well what you probably mean is you want to become a programmer what does that look like well you probably want to be able to get a job as a programmer one of those k yr silicon valley jobs you keep hearing about okay what will it take to get there perhaps the plan isn t all that obvious there are a million things to do along the way and no obvious order to do them do you have to learn python what about c how much what does it mean to know python what do you want to be able to do with it well what would an employer expect you to be able to do with it don t know well there s a good thing to start with let s find out what employers expect and then go figure out how to be able to do those things what about watching a youtube video from somebody showing you how to make an ai agent to play snake hmm it s not obvious how that really helps you are you really learning how to do it or are you just watching a video could you make your own after watching that i guess you d better at least try or else it was almost certainly not worth it set concrete goals for yourself every day every week every month at the end of the period reflect on how well you achieved that goal if you find your goals are unreachable scale them back make sure it s something you can point at and definitively say yes i did accomplish that or no i have not yet completed that learn python become a programmer at what point can you definitively say you have done those things maybe you need something more concrete this is where projects are useful be clear about what the project should become and what it should do let s make up a concrete goal let s make our snake ai agent i want to build an ai agent for a game of snake the game is played on a x grid the agent must take inputs from the current state of the world and eat apples and grow without running into a wall or itself it will start at a length of segments and gain a segment every time it eats an apple i expect it will be able to grow up to a length of segments without hitting itself or a wall and to do this consistently i won t make my own snake game i will use lt insert existing implementation gt i plan to finish this by the end of the month great now at the end of the month i can look back and confidently say how much of that i ve accomplished have i done it all what s missing do i have a plan of how to continue how much time will that take now i m updating my imperfect plan based on where i have found myself but a month is a long time for a goal daily goals are much easier to plan easier to accomplish and give you many more opportunities to practise making and evaluating them here are some daily goals i ve made in the past today i am going to make lunch and supper i won t buy fast food today i am going to plan out my meals in advance and make sure they fit within my calorie goals i will not eat anything not on the plan and i will not make a plan that exceeds my calorie goals today i am going to go to the gym and do each of the exercises in the recovery plan that my physiotherapist gave me today i am going to investigate ticket and figure out what is happening and what is causing it i don t expect to have a solution to it by the end of the day but i plan to by the end of the week today i am going to read the next chapter of my book today i am going to go to bed by pm today i am going to cut the lawn some of these might seem silly but honestly how many times have you said to yourself i didn t find the time to cut the lawn or aww man i stayed up way too late again last night i m sure you were thinking of all the other things you would rather be doing but if you make a concrete plan to do it and continually evaluate where you are in your plan you ll find it much harder to ignore those things you know you should do you ll have to consciously acknowledge your actions and justify why you aren t following your plan just that conscious thought is often all it takes to keep ourselves on track so long as we hold ourselves to them there is nothing magic about making goals and plans setting aside minutes every morning to think through your day isn t automatically going to make you stay focused you still need to put in considerable mental effort and be disciplined but these particular things provide a framework a plan there are a million things you could try but these are things that successful people do that work for them so if you re going to put in effort to do something it s probably best to try doing these things', 'by experience through highschool and college i will try to keep this brief as possible i think i am a bit slow at learning new skills or putting new information in memory first noticed this during my al days i would not understand anything taught in school but will get good grades in final exams this kinda baffled my teachers who think i never pay attention in class the real problem was that i never learned things at the phase they taught in school at home i could read and practice a concept over and over again until it solidifies this problem was not only for academic learning when i go to any class to learn a new skill eg martial arts rowing driving etc i had to repeat moves many number of times than anyone else in the class the instructors would complain that i don t pay enough attention but i do pay my maximum attention it s just that i have to repeat it that many times apart from that i don t remember directions at all i cannot remember how to go to a place even if i go there a hundred times has to use google maps to go anywhere those days i was tested with a psychologist for learning disabilities but they said they couldn t diagnose me with a recognized disorder like dyslexia anway i ended up with a s and a b for core mathematics biology chemistry and physics for my al s this was after tremendous effort since i had to study things over and over again because i will forget them quickly afterwards i was too burned out and decided to not do science or tech for college and went in for a finance degree the workload was manageable but after one month i hated it at the same time i followed the coursera course learning how to learn by dr babara oakley it teaches how many people are capable of doing stem subjects with proper study techniques so after some research i switched to a cs major along the way i learned plenty of study skills that made life bearable for example anki flashcard software lets me put something in memory i dabbled in mnemonics but didn t find much success there for example i can t use loci technique because i don t remember locations to begin with my experience with learning programming i worked for months in a company as an intern during my third year the main advice i got both in person and online about learning frameworks was not to try to memorize anything in programming also don t try to make notes just refer to the documentation when needed you will automatically memorize the libraries you are working with most often but the issue with me is that i never learn anything like that unless i actively try to commit it to memory so against common advice i decided to memorize programming syntax with anki my anki decks however this became too overwhelming there were too many cards and i saw that there was no point in memorizing something that i only use rarely so i changed my way i put to anki only the fundementals of a library framework or things that will be needed frequently rest of the things i put into methodical onenote notebooks for example this is my notebook section on ndarrays in numpy library onenote notes about ndarrays again someone would argue that this is a waste of time i could always refer to the documentation rather than making these notes but for me this is what works having things organized in a way that makes sense to me and this is my notebook on machine learning onenote notes about machine learning this is the notebook i used to record things in my last project at work onenote notebook about work project self doubt all my life i was full of self doubt that mainly stemmed from my learning difficulties to sum up i don t learn things easily by naturally doing a task i have to actively commit things to memory using techniques like flashcards or maintaining methodical notebooks both these are considered ineffective in programming i somehow manage to get the tasks done at work although with methods that are considered weird by others in the field would like some feedback are there others like me who have to make notes or actively memorize things is this practice sustainable in the long run x b x b x b x b x b x b x b x b x b x b x b x b available each year the more marketable skills you have the more employable and marketable you are law is nothing like what you see in movies and tv shows it is long boring hours of researching
