eventually choose one or two of these interests and pursue them and leave the others as side interests machine learning is mostly about statistics with a bit of vector calculus thrown in and some higher level stuff at the research level like generalized linear modeling ml doesn t really have much of anything to do with ethics ai research on the other hand note that ai and ml are different things does deal with some psychology and will often borrow concepts from psychology a lot i second u tony t s reading recommendations your ethical interests will probably have to be left as a hobby though because unless you re in a position of some influence there isn t really a clear way to act on them yuval harari is an example of such a person you can add lessons for the st century to your reading list i ve heard good things it sounds like you may enjoy psychology neuroscience research i would recommend contacting some professors at a nearby university who are doing research now and Name: 67_conferences_journals_paper_arxiv
Representative_Docs: ['great post and a valuable read i ve saved it for future reference i m currently fourth year student at a university that doesn t offer phd s sister campus to the main campus i did some undergraduate research myself in my third year with machine learning and natural language processing with a couple of professors the research wasn t funded or active research and was more or less side projects that one of my professors wanted to work on i worked on text classification with neural networks and found some results that rivaled a couple of papers i learned a ton in terms of theory frameworks and general research best practices i ve written a couple of papers to go along with the work nothing groundbreaking in all honestly when i entered it into a workshop it was brutally reviewed and rejected i think what came down to it was my professors disagreeing on what was best for the paper in terms of what should be written and if something should be cited or not i should also mention that my professors more or less were mentors for my questions and didn t work on the research itself it was almost like a course project where they were only available for questions if i had them and that was that the research work was fully conducted by me including code and papers after the rejection i gave a month break to it to pay attention to my heavy load of courses for spring quarter of my rd year now i am thinking of looking back at the paper and try my hand at other workshops and conferences if that doesn t fall through at best i think these papers are best for arxiv this summer i ve been working on an independent study with a friend to learn more about rnns gans and attention based models and applying our knowledge to some algo trading strategies not exactly research but i wrote a paper about what i ve learned and some basic theory my question do you think someone like me with papers on arxiv is still something great for grad school did i start too late i ve seen some undergraduate students with first name or second name on respectable conferences like cvpr or nips but none of my particular university professors are working on developing cutting edge algorithms starting to wish i knew more about research earlier in my university career and maybe i could ve sought out professors from the main campus', 'just fyi since i haven t seen it mentioned already in cs the premier venues for peer reviewed publications are conferences not journals link to see the top conferences for individual subfields eg ai check csrankings org however i m still a high school student and haven t written any paper on algorithm and coding stuffs yet also does anyone know any journals that i can submit this honestly just make a github repo with a nice readme don t put it on arxiv the paper is likely to be bad and you probably don t want it permanently associated with your name on a platform meant for hosting high quality preprints you can t delete stuff from arxiv not to mention you ll need a university email or reference to submit to arxiv in the first place', 'where do you go to university what journals are you looking at i work in your field and i promise you re wrong about fees you should not be publishing in journals that charge you to publish they are almost exclusively predatory or journals of low repute some journals charge excess page fees but that s usually only if you re paper is longer than their desired length you should also look at conferences which in our field are peer reviewed and as important if not more than journals conferences charge attendance fees but that s very different than a publishing fee if you can t get the attendance fee covered by your university you can apply for scholarships and similar to cover the costs try looking at international journal of data science and analytics international conference on data science and advanced analytics international conference on data science and information technology acs ieee international conference on computer systems and applications those are ones that immediately come to mind that are data science specific however you should also look at publishing in conferences and journals about artificial intelligence databases and other fields related to your work if you do data science work based in another field like political science logistics urban planning etc you should publish work in journals and conferences in those fields as well']

math based programs i want to build something different python is great for machine learning math and automation but if you want to create an app how do you go about it there has to be some sort of software development guide that s universal for every language and teaches the concepts', 'gt i want to start by learning python why are you so sure about this i also started with python because years ago i thought i wanted to be a data scientist turns out i didn t really understand what that was about and learning python is not nearly enough to get there now python is pretty easy and for a coding novice a good introduction to programming but depending on what you want to do python can range from mildly useful to completely useless because it s such a high level language people rarely program whole apps using python and if you re looking into data science its far more valuable to put time into learning statistics and math to support machine learning rather than python if you re a web developer python is useful for backend development because django is one of the premier backend frameworks so this is all to say figure out what you want to do with it first because likely aside from getting your feet wet with easy programming python is probably not going to set you apart enough to lead to a career change graduating a bootcamp and being good at python raises the question in what context and if you can t prove value in a context that s familiar to the interviewer you will go nowhere or worse you ll end up being an instructor at the bootcamp amp x b there are several bootcamps relatively new data science curriculums and by my last searches not many had data on the placement rates because the programs were so young generally bootcamps really shine in the software web development side of it and not so much the data science programs and again then are hard my friend owns a startup tech company that build a web scraper and he s hired several people from flatiron school he said that the guy he hired talked alot about the attrition which doesn t get reported in the official numbers because if you read the fine print the way they count these percentages of people who pass the course there are a ton of qualifiers like students have to not miss a single class and must complete all weeks with all projects submitted must submit x number of applications for jobs per day after graduation or else they don t count that person in the denominator of the who graduated and are now employed so when you see of students are not employed in tech positions that usually means of all the people who made it through the course and jumped through the hoops and spent several weeks of submitting applications per day of those students got hired anyway rant over just be careful op']

Name: 76_economics_science_masters_data science
Representative_Docs: ['gt i m undecided as to whether i want to be a swe or branch more into machine learning data science which my econ background would predispose me to but i ve found several mscs courses that would let me take electives in stats ml and keep both options open i don t know how it works in the uk but in the us a masters in economics is perfectly acceptable for data analysis science roles economics is a data driven social science look at people like raj chetty for instance i ve met data scientists who came from economics backgrounds so it s not that uncommon you will literally see job postings that explicitly state something like an advanced degree in math cs statistics and economics or related preferred a ms in cs will be more flexible in terms of career opportunities but this is just something to consider as to your questions i feel like your application will be much stronger for mscs programs with some cs coursework and letters from cs professors take cs classes put up some projects if you can to show that you are interested and that you can actually program tech is full of people working as programmers without a cs degree the thing about tech is that it s grown so fast that it s pretty common to see non cs people working as programmers i don t think anyone really bats an eye anymore but usually most jobs want to see some kind of quantitative degree you might as well send out some applications it doesn t hurt and if you haven t tried there s only one way to find out', 'so i am currently and i have a bs in mathematics followed by a year of experience in the analytics industry my long term career goal is to be working in the private sector as a data science professional i am currently facing a dilemma about what masters program i should be applying for so many of my friends have gone for ms in data science that to me it seems like mimicking the crowd at this point moreover these programs have mostly popped up quite recently so there is no real consensus over the syllabus or the program duration since data science encompasses math stats computer science databases and business studies how is it even realistic to expect a year ms program to provide an in depth understanding of all these diverse fields to me it seems like these programs would offer a clumsy amalgamation of the aforementioned subjects only to churn out data science professionals who know all the buzzwords and have a lot of surface level knowledge without any real depth or expertise does the industry really need more of that on the other hand while a traditional ms in stats would provide a thorough training in the statistics component it fails to address all of the others can someone really learn cs business studies and databases all by themselves while pursuing a full time ms furthermore if i do a phd in stats would i not alienate myself from these fields even more and make my skill set too narrow and rigid to adapt to industry requirements i m sure people here have taken both routes and managed to find satisfaction in their careers what would you say are the benefits or drawbacks of an ms in data science as compared to an ms in statistics is a phd only open after ms stats or do ms data science folks also explore that possibility how big of a competitive advantage does a phd give', 'i m very close to getting my degree in economics but i always had a passion for cs i m mostly interested in software engineering data science and anything ai related but i have no idea how to proceed from here i already have some parent s and they are ok with it but it will take me another years at the very least and that s my main problem with this option getting a master s in data science i can also do this in my city for a small tuition also a public uni and be done in a year and a half they do accept economics grads the problem with this choice is that i can only ever be a data scientist analyst i really wouldn t like an economics career x b i get very mixed responses from people when i ask for advice on this dilemma some people condemn masters with data science on the title as data science is supposed to be a job title not a degree title others tell me that going for the master s is my best and quickest bet as i ll be close to when i finish my second bachelor s and that s not good for my career when i asked a professor for advice he argued that i ll have a difficult time getting a job as employers will prefer people with a cs bsc for the same role and i should just follow an economics career or do a second bachelor s i m really confused to the point of depression and i could really use some guidance thanks']

Name: 77_msc_marketing_ve_analyst
Representative_Docs: ['hi everyone so i m in my second year of university in the uk i m currently doing a bsc in accounting and finance i ve realised that i don t want to do accounting or audit after i finish i ve been looking into project management and consulting but honestly haven t made up my mind on what i want to do i ve applied to a number of internships that sounded somewhat interesting like economic consulting but have had very little luck with getting beyond the video interview stages i m quite mindful of the developments in ai and the potential for automation in future and it s made me dubious about looking at careers like mortgage advisory i worked previously as a procurement intern and enjoyed automating a lot of manual entry processes i m quite skilled at excel and enjoy analysing data and identifying trends however i could improve at this as i do not have as much knowledge as say someone from a cs or data science background i would prefer a role that is intellectually stimulating and where i can work with others basically what advice could you give in terms of opportunities i should look into and how to secure a job after graduating thanks', 'i m passionate about entomology this led to me studying a msc in wildlife biology and conservation which i graduated from last year a year or so previous i graduated with a bsc hons in molecular genetics there are few jobs in entomology in the uk and i ve realised in the past year or so that the idea of monetising ones passion for an institution business really doesn t resonate with me i d rather do it on the side without any deadlines or a pay cheque attached to it even so the sorts of jobs i could go for after my msc were ecological consulting positions which were contractual unreliable inconsistent low paying k and hyper competitive lots of previous voluntary experience required i instead took a service desk analyst position at my uni which has a much better salary k and a decent environment with colleagues i get on well with more important to me than money prestige and the sort of daily tasks the job requires we ve got a new boss things are still alright but the office environment has declined a little i don t necessarily want to move position now but i d like to expand my prospects for the future so if when i need to move on when the payscale tops out or the environment gets too bad it ll be easier more likely i ll get a better paying job in a field with lots of available opportunities the only opportunities available to me at my current position are in a second line team like desktops networks or web team and gaining experience for this would mean simply working with them every wednesday which doesn t do much for my cv broadly speaking i want to add to my cv to improve my employability and legitimise my working in an it field i ve considered doing a postgraduate diploma in computing with the open university i m not sure about some of the software engineering modules i ve only used r as a programming tool for some statistics in my msc and it seems to require quite a bit of java experience i m open to taking on some undergrad modules this year to work up to this i think a pgdip is more cost effective than a full msc as the third and final year of a msc in computing with the ou is simply a research project which i feel is an unnecessary cost k given a computing project only really requires a computer and doesn t require a lab or any specialised equipment that said if after getting the pgdip and searching for jobs i find that employers really want the full msc i d happily go back and pay the extra k do the research project and get a msc other information online forums and other subreddits etc seem to recommend free online courses moocs lynda udemy coursera linkedin learning etc or paid certifications comptia itil but i m concerned these don t really appeal to employers that much do employers for data science software engineering sysadmin really care about itil comptia over a postgrad degree some forum posts recommend creating a coding portfolio github to showcase coding skills and ability x b all in all i d really appreciate a bit of advice on what to do next to be more employable in it cheers', 'ba in statistics is nothing to be embarrassed about great major but it s totally fine that you also didn t enjoy the degree super transferrable so i don t think there s any need to worry apologies if what i write below is something you ve considered already just my perspective based on experience that is similar to yours and years post grad feeling totally lost also i really know nothing about stats so sorry if any of this is presumptuous i think what is really tough about job hunting when you re not anchored by your degree discipline is having a lack of focus or guardrails to look within it s so difficult to find a job when the job hunting is not super targeted so maybe have a think about what are the things i enjoy doing on a day to day basis what kinds of activities do can you imagine doing every day e g talking to people making things categorising things measuring things editing checking things what are my general interests no matter how abstract or niche they are e g gaming ai porcelain dolls nunchucks what things am i good at e g are you good enough with numbers data to make it through a degree you are definitely a ton better than the average person are you good with directions organised is logical thinking required for my degree just list all of those things and there definitely will be some related to how you finished college these questions will help you identify some areas that you could be successful in just by googling careers that need those skills or jobs in your areas of interest or jobs that involved doing your day to day then you can find out if a course whether it s at community college or on linkedin learning or udemy is what you need to kind of give you a leg up into a career e g if you re interest in porcelain dolls you could help start to aggregate data in the market for which dolls are most popular at particular times of the year for a company that makes them you could look up data analysis for for sales on udemy or something i didn t want to suggest some career ideas for you since i m not sure what you re into e g you might detest the idea of marketing or think working in education or it is a route to a slow death but here are some examples data analyst like someone else says really clear link between stats and this job role even if you don t know that much stats marketing marketing research good salary prospects requires some good communications empathy skills however from the way you ve written your post seems like you re great at this anyway hci interested in tech being able to use your brain in a particular way that stats requires is really relevant here i think that s also the key rather than the specific skills you need to learn think about how a degree in stats requires you to think e g logical attention to detail lastly consider picking up knowledge with particular software related to stats software experts by expert i just mean someone who knows more than the other people in the room are always in demand google the most used and go from them there hope this advice wasn t totally useless']

Representative_Docs: ['there are a few choices you have getting started firstly are you allowed to use existing machine learning libraries and frameworks or are you expected to interpret and build the algorithms yourself i will assume it is the first case otherwise you will need a much deeper understanding and considerably more time to achieve this goal amp x b machine learning itself is a huge a field so to help narrow down your search a little there are a couple of families of approaches within machine learning which i would recommend looking into unsupervised learning this approach focuses on grouping similar items together by comparing similarities in your case you could use your questionnaire answers to group people for example people who watch movies like the notebook and the fault in our stars could be grouped by into a romantic drama category a good algorithm to get started with here is k nearest neighbour supervised learning this approach tries to categorise items into predefined categories and learns from a data set which has example items labelled with which category they belong in in your case you could have one question in your questionnaire be what are your favourite categories of movies this would be the labels for that persons data set then any movies which they list they like can be associated with those categories a good starting place here would be to look in artificial neural networks amp x b whichever machine learning approach you choose you will need data to train and test your network this has to be a pretty large data set if you want to get any good results and is probably going to be your biggest barrier to trying to achieve this the suggested approaches for your problem are also simplified and won t necessarily be great ways to categorise your data amp x b if you are allowed to use existing machine learning libraries and frameworks here are a few i would recommend they can all implement the unsupervised and supervised learning methods mentioned above keras a high level machine learning library with good support built of google s tensorflow tensorflow the foundation of keras allows more in depth tweaking and control if you need a more bespoke solution azure this is microsofts machine learning offering i ve not used it myself but i ve heard good things amp x b let me know if you have any more specific questions', 'honestly you should consider using the google maps api per requests with a free credit each month is free requests every month which is a lot unless you are rolling out this service to tons of users or requesting travel times every minute additionally you could cache results to cut down drastically on the number of requests that need to be made store the results of every request in a database of your own and then when your app needs to get the travel time first check to see if a similar route has already been requested and if so provide that possibly with adjustments for time of day year weather etc otherwise make the request to google maps you could also build on that by doing some dead reckoning to make each travel time more usable so if there is some stored route a gt b and someone requests a route from say a mile away from a to b then add or subtract some rough time estimate based on how much closer or farther they are from b additionally you can chain routes together sometimes if you have a route a gt b a route b gt c and someone requests a gt c and b is roughly in between a and c then you can return a gt b b gt c and that will get you pretty close if you really don t want to use the google maps api consider one of the openstreetmaps apis and if you really don t want to do that then i guess you could roll your own but i m not kidding when i say that it will be x more complicated than using someone s api if this app is at all a practical idea and not just for fun it makes zero business sense to try to roll your own in this field but anyways first you need data specifically road map data and speed limit data openstreetmaps has that data for free i think then you need a way to take gps data and convert it to points on roads then you need to use a weighted graph traversal algorithm google it to find the best route to your destination where the edges are road segments and the weights are distances speed limits once you have the best route you need to calculate the traverse time for each segment in your route and sum them to get the total travel time if you want to get fancy you can apply a modifier to this based on time of year day weather etc this could be a machine learning thing but so far it would be the only part that is you might be able to find some sort of machine learning y graph traversal set up but i don t think it s strictly necessary the alternative to that is pure machine learning gather gps data about where your users are at different points in time then train an ml system to predict travel times without knowing anything about roads or speed limits this is extremely impractical the issue is training an ml model requires a lot of data points scaled by the complexity of the system i think you d need millions and millions data points before you started seeing any useful results and up until that point most of the predictions will be garbage', 'salutations i had an idea for a program that i d like to write Name: 81_unity_game_graphics_opengl
Representative_Docs: ['i don t think you should need anything particularly advanced in terms of python knowledge to use a library like pygame it should mostly just be calling functions and maybe using lists and dictionaries which you already seem to know if a library did require some other more advanced feature of python that would give you a good reason and motivation to learn that feature so you d understand what you learn better anyway using unity is actually pretty easy for the most part and that s part of the selling point though you might want to look into the godot engine instead as a free open source alternative you should be able to create full d games with these without much technical skill though understanding programming will help a lot but it seems like you already understand enough to do something useful these programs are largely geared towards artists and designers who don t know much programming or who don t want to deal with as much programming though you can get pretty complex with writing code in unity including writing your own custom shaders which requires rather advanced skill and understanding with d math and d graphics the other main alternative to creating a d game with something like unreal unity or godot is to write most of it yourself in code probably using something like c or maybe c with gpu libraries like opengl direct d or vulkan using libraries like opengl does not strictly require but benefits greatly from understanding the math and theory behind d graphics there are also a large number of other slightly more niche subtopics data structures and algorithms which you d need to know to do it yourself you should probably implement some kind of bsp like an octree you basically need to write your own physics and collision detection your own file loading functions to load images like jpegs fonts d models sounds and manage the simulation and the objects in your world possibly using an ecs or just your own run of the mill inheritance based system you probably need to learn some ai algorithms with specific usefulness to games such as dijkstra a and various techniques such as occlusion culling backface culling emissive shaders and a ton of other things you d probably need to write your own networking code if you want to handle multiplayer and this is not going to be trivial to learn to do well you probably need some decent background in concurrency and parallelism like using threads but this is not strictly required and many high performance d games are still single threaded and the gpu takes care of most of this performance boost by handling parallel processing for you much of this also applies to writing your own d game though there are fewer things to learn in a sufficiently complex d game you still might benefit from an ecs or bsp but you d use a quadtree instead of an octree if you re writing your own d game yourself you probably would still need to write your own networking collision detection physics and ai pathfinding code etc don t let this discourage you i would highly encourage anyone to try to write their own game engine if it s of interest but it s also a matter of what s important to you if you want to spend your time actually making a game you should probably just use an existing game engine if the idea of implementing your own physics simulation bsp ecs fbx and dds loaders Name: 82_recruiters_recruiter_company_interview
Representative_Docs: ['ok this may be a bit of an overreaction specially because these incidents were early on in the application process but i am extremely frustrated by the lack of maturity most recruiters and hr people i deal with have so i will name and shame them sure i may be a chopped liver but i hate when they waste my fucking time i applied for both these companies here in toronto my experience very closely matches the job requirements so i got prompt emails from recruiters to arrange the initial chats first comes the magic leap recruiter this guy send me an email telling me how excited the team is to talk to me and asks me to submit a few time slots which i do in the reply he confirms a completely different time than i had suggested so i just play along and double confirm on the day off he ends up calling me at the original time i suggested i make some excuses to get out of work to talk to him and he asks me if i am willing to move to florida i say that the job posting was for fucking toronto and i do not want to move to florida he goes ok cool cool the scheduling assistant will get in touch soon to arrange another tech screen i m like awesome the scheduling assistant never contacts me the same job along with tonnes of other jobs get re posted every two weeks and i never hear back from the recruiter i was not even expecting it anyway second comes autonomic i work for a company that is in the same space as them working with a lot of tech they use i get an email from a recruiter telling me they are interested i get passed on to a scheduling assistant to arrange this call we go back and forth times to decide a time then we close off with a confirmation and good lucks on the day off i make an excuse to go in late to work to take this call because its winter and i get no privacy at work no one calls i send the initial contact and email to see what s up and get met with absolute silence the same position gets posted over and over almost every two weeks i just want a little bit of respect as a job applicant just be mature and learn to communicate even their fucking job descriptions require it', 'recruiters are there to feast on making links between a company that doesn t know how to look for stuff and potential employees that don t know how to address companies directly or simply aren t looking for stuff they demand a lot of time from a lot of potential employees making full profiles with long discussions so that they could place you in a matching engine you gotta understand how they re making their money they want as many profiles as possible as complete as possible with as low expectations as possible then they can make matches the usual strategy is contact person x tell them you work with big company and are basically best friends with the ceo of some cool company that has just received over in funding and is evaluated at already and growing fast and they need you because you re so special and you re a perfect fit according to the profile and they can pay top buck for you equivalent of median salary in your field set up a call at this step they really want to maximize the response rate it s a big market only a few recruiters get answered some companies will likely have a b tests set up machine learning nlp just to form proper messages that lure candidates in call and talk with you in the middle discover something that you re lacking everybody lacks something and say well there is a company i have that would really want someone who has that but there s another one we can talk about while gathering a lot more stuff from you that is completely irrelevant from the original requirements of the company ask for your current salary and your expectations the median salary in your field is gone that s some other company at most if you re really good you can talk your way through median salary in your field or maybe more if you re already earning more otherwise this conversation would be over promise to hook you up with this cool company that has a solution to world hunger and is on the verge of curing cancer with their new product that is going to be a booming success end call never hear from the recruiter ever again if your expectations are too high honestly i m a sought after developer i get tons of messages from companies and recruiters last time after a start up went bust i had to cancel my vacation trips in the month after because there were companies offering me a lot of money if i started earlier i couldn t stay unemployed without burning bridges and after that it took me exactly week between starting answering messages on linkedin to actually getting a contract with more pay and a much more interesting activity i m not the kind of person that has trouble getting hired yet i ve spent a cumulative h on call with recruiters in the last years and only one a single one ended up with a set up interview at a meh it s allright company and then they went all crazy on me when i canceled the interview due to another contract being signed the best strategy for me is to deal with the companies directly i ve gotten offers from of the companies that i ve interacted with directly the rest were either not a good fit with my skills i botched the interview had too high expectations appeared too late and they already hired someone etc and just one of many recruiters set me up with an actual interview no disrespect to recruiters they may be helpful to some people and it s just people making a buck it s not like i m a saint or anything it s just that from my experience the last thing recruiters want is to up your expectations after the call they need a contact a contract and commissions and your high expectations is something hard to work with for them', 'i have searched tech jobs twice in my life once as a new grad in and just now for those that are just curious or for those that are older and am curious about the current recruitment process let me explain what i saw jobs were super easy to get it was a weird time when non tech folks were in charge of tech folks also the amount of technology used wasn t as massive and varied as it is now no one asked for years of python experience with computer vision with jenkins within a docker container or whatever because that shit didn t exist back then it was a much simpler time it was kind of development of system admin as the major pillars back then this meant that often times it was behavioral and simple questions as many hiring managers were just general people managers and not engineering managers in terms of tech questioning whiteboarding of useless problems was the only way to test really but it wasn t that complicated and if you were decent and communicated well you got the job i think i ended up with offers out of second round interviews i got rejected by one but another one gave me two offers but since i just finished undergrad silly algo data structure problems were all i knew it was super easy for me sure the first time i saw vi i was scared and had to ask a colleague what this was but i could traverse a graph on whiteboard like a motherfucker recruiting was also different it was put your resume in a resume database and kind of wait job fairs were the best way to do that the massive recruiting teams that large employers have now were definitely not at today s scale this meant that you got fewer requests for jobs but you also weren t competing against other people for that one position essentially if you were contacted there was a much better chance you were getting the job due to limited hr resources it saved a lot of time also there were no tiered awesome companies with great pay it was pretty standard for a new grad i got k and a few piddly stock options at the time at the most awesome company ever a company that would never run out of ideas and dominate the industry forever that company was sun microsystems so yeah don t count on me for any gambling advice pretty much ever company was the within k of that with varying degrees of stock options all that being said the fallout of the dot com bust one year later was dramatic all those people who were hired with limited credentials and skills suddenly got canned and things got tight suddenly knowing html didn t make you a coder anymore i know a lot of people who were plain screwed there were no bootcamps back then but equivalent were the people that learned to code with the learn java in days books were assed out at the end of the day a lot of them went it to real estate so yeah you can put two and two together on what the next downturn was first thing first holy fucking shit job searches are annoying you need to match all these random technologies then even if you have that you have to memorize all those leetcode tricks that s right not skills but tricks sure i know loops and trees and the like but dang i didn t remember the trick to get the consecutive subset of numbers to equal a passed in sum efficiently mine was inefficient so yeah even though i matched pretty darn well with the job requirements i did not get that coding parlor trick so i m out this was for a partner engineering position btw which in no way shape or form would require any sort of algorithmic knowledge in my undergrad days i would say i memorized of those tricks out there today i know about so i was immediately knocked out of like of interviews i didn t realize that the leetcode monkey dance would be so prevalent next job search i know what to study for this last one i was ill prepared anyway i think most people felt the algo data structures problems were outdated years ago but man they are even worse now but knowing the trick basically got me an in as well so yeah it s completely fucking random whether i impress people or not one company thinks i m an idiot and nother thought i was god because of the random selection of leetcode esqe questions on the opposite end holy fucking shit does this pay well my fucking god years ago those that got k were lucky to jump in the right company at the right time with the right options were a super genius someone who is some major thought leader or some senior director now a schmuck like me can get near k this is crazy i joined a company for k in in total compensation and i was ecstatic in i think i was rightly paid at k or something like that now i just accepted an offer for k this is nice but also a bit scary i ve been through different downturns what s going to happen if there s another downturn and these crazy salaries whither away let me put it another way for the early to mid s my wife and i were paid the same though she s way smarter than me but since she does supply chain and not tech she s gotten about a increase in pay in the last years pretty good and my pay has roughly doubled i m also amazed that some companies out there think that it is still and offer those salaries most non tech companies are completely flabbergasted in terms of my desired salary many of them came back later with a substantial increase because they couldn t find anyone qualified but i still had to say it wasn t enough Name: 85_title_intern_titles_role
Representative_Docs: ['i m a data scientist the title can mean a lot of different things at a lot of different companies at my current company i started off as a glorified data analyst mostly writing sql queries and creating dashboards but due to significant turnover i had to take over the data engineering side of things which ended up being really valuable experience and also i discovered i really like coding and i m pretty ok at it so these days i mostly do data engineering stuff dabble in machine learning on the side and a lot of time spent attending meetings and managing projects but that s besides the point but my title hasn t changed at all despite my day to day job being totally different as well as the technologies i use on a daily basis also most data scientist job postings i see require either a phd or equivalent work experience which translates to that years of experience you re seeing for junior devs the entry level jobs are often titled data analyst which might not require much work experience but at least at my company they also get leveled lower than junior devs to start with', 'most places have hr systems that use an enum for their official titles which means they change slowly if at all then they have a text field that s got your actual title it s usual to have at least two titles because of that often at a place that does project based work you ll have more i have at least six titles that i m known by depending on who s talking and i ll use whichever one matches best on my resumes the only place the differences should cause a problem is if you list say sr vp and their system says jr or intern then it ll make you look like a liar if you re really concerned you list both titles machine learning intern associate', 'i m planning on taking a job at a company where i ll be doing a mix of machine learning and full stack development i have fairly extensive experience in both areas but it s been developed largely through solo contracting work and i don t really have any standard cs oriented positions on my resume this has led me to be somewhat concerned about what my title will be because i feel like it could have a significant impact on my future career direction amp x b the company suggested lead data scientist as my title but they said that i could choose lead machine learning engineer senior software engineer or a similar position within reason i ve anecdotally heard from friends at big companies that data scientist positions tend to make less money that full stack engineers i tried backing this up with research online but it s difficult to separate out other factors like experience level it also seems reasonable that the senior software engineer would look better when seeking out a full stack position while the others might look better when seeking out a more data oriented position amp x b my question boils down to how these different titles might impact future salaries and job opportunities any input that anybody has would be much appreciated']

Representative_Docs: ['you might find this useful personally i d advise you against being prescriptive regarding the tech stack if you hire the right people they should be trusted to make that machine learning engineers data engineers i say this because you are going to need someone who can actually build the bot and moreover before the ai guys can build useful things you are going to need data with this said refer back to the nd paragraph don t be too perspective instead find people who know how to build teams', 'hello i am an investor trader seeking information about machine learning i have plans funds to start building a trading bot at the moment i am doing the research needed to do this unfortunately my programming skills are nowhere near the level of expertise to pull off such a complicated task that is building a machine learning trading bot x b that is why i have funds so i can work with programming experts but i m in a bit of a pickle right now because i can t seem to find any information regarding what type of personnel i would need to pull this off i know i need people who are proficient in the framework s of my liking i ve decided to use python and tensorflow x b what i seek is information regarding the number of people i should have working on this project and what type of professionals i should work with to pull this off as of now i ve come up with a team of programmers and a computer scientist or data scientist my question is should i include a mathematician with that group or computer data scientist are enough to pull this off x b i want to thank anyone in advance who responds to this post', 'gt i don t know much about go some people actually think it s just as badly made as php lol i ve never heard that actually haha all that i ve heard is that it s one of the best languages that experienced developers have ever worked in it has its issues for sure but from what i understand it s extremely well built and the services built in go have decidedly lower rates of bugs failing tests in our company gt and what is aws really do if your website just gets by fine with shared web hosting ohhhhhhh my duuuuuudddddddeeeee it s amazing come join us in r devops at my company we use terraform to declaratively define all of our infrastructure for two systems our machine learning service and our development infrastructure our machine learning service has some dumb autoscaling where we have a jenkins job check the depth of a rabbitmq queue every few minutes and then tell aws to spin up a bunch more servers and workers if it crosses a certain threshold it is one of our most demanding services in terms of performance but because of the beauty of autoscaling and the cost effectiveness of aws it s also one of our cheapest to run our development infrastructure is a set of aws instances running a docker swarm cluster in which we deploy ephemeral environments for qa and integration testing purposes the entire cost of our dev infrastructure is a few thousand a month and because of aws amp terraform if something goes wrong with it we just destroy the existing one and spin up another our upcoming project that i m most excited about is to migrate over to kubernetes and set up a cluster in aws that our devs can deploy ephemeral environments to as a part of their normal workflow using skaffold garden or another similar tool then extend that functionality to move our integration testing over to it as well we ll then be able to autoscale that entire cluster and have our costs significantly reduced by just scaling up servers based upon demand and then hopefully integrate cool tools like chaos monkey if we re lucky honestly the chicago market is great if you know where to look there are so many fantastic startups here that pay extremely well i m making k at this current place and i was making k at my previous and are also using cutting edge technology that plus the low cost of living compared to ny seattle la or sf makes it a really appealing city to be a software dev in imo']

Name: 91_mathy_math_jobs_require phd
Representative_Docs: ['i can tell you as a math major who have been in the workforce a few years that most jobs that don t require a phd simply aren t that mathematical i really wish this was not the case but it is even the jobs that are considered mathematical are mostly about trying to code the math into the computer and you have libraries that does a lot of heavy lifting for you the math you use most likely isn t that advanced like matrix multiplication or linear regression ols math is all done on computers now and i would argue that this actually makes it quite important to know things like cloud computing servers etc because this is where your computer will do the math nobody is doing it by hand outside of the ivory towers you will also never have to prove anything those are for the phds literally no company is hiring fresh out of school newcomers to do pde or topological data analysis which is an actual thing data science and machine learning is like cleaning manipulating wrangling data i ve learned with a lot of disappointment that even with master s you are competing with phds for many of these advanced math jobs which automatically puts you at a handicap without the doctoral degree however i have heard that quant finance is more open to non phd people now although you still have a lot of quant jobs that are specifically geared towards math physics phds i ve also heard that supply chain and operations is increasingly computerized and algorithm driven with all the data now', 'gt even my side dabbling in machine learning doesn t really require much since all the algorithms are already written if you re using a lib like tensorflow although i m sure ml gets more mathy when you get more advanced with it yeah i have a friend that works in ml and he s also told me that it s hardly mathy i think a lot of people here think it s mathy because all these theoretical classes you take in university are math but once you actually work in industry you have these libraries or services that does the ml for you written by people with phds the math is taken care of so to speak gt what jobs in cs get into heavy calculus linear algebra etc on the reg i have a bachelor s degree in math and there really aren t that much mathy jobs that don t explicitly require a phd i asked a similar question on r math a while back and that was kind of the consensus as well as accurate from my own experience i too wish this was not the case but it is i think the most relatively mathy jobs as you can get without a phd are probably not in any particular order finance quant data scientist or actuary', 'i have a bachelor s degree in math and to be frank there really aren t many mathy jobs out in industry even the few ones that are mathy require a phd i truly wish this was not the case but it is gt is academia the right outlet for such interests if you really really want to do math then yes academia has interesting jobs gt what about industry or ai as i said before very very few truly mathy jobs if you don t have a phd even jobs in ai aren t mathy in the sense that you are not gonna be doing delta epsilon proofs from real analysis nor will you be doing anything advanced from linear algebra maybe a matrix multiplication which you will do with python but using numpy is hardly considered math by math students but most mathematical computations are done with libraries and software these days that s the point of these tools you don t want to re invent the wheel for example i would never implement linear regression by hand when i can just use lm in r same thing with deep learning stuff with tensorflow or pytorch whether you want to count a deep learning job that uses these libraries as math is another argument some people may consider that a math job i personally don t there are operations jobs where you can use operations research concepts like integer linear programming but again there s software like gurobi that do a lot of the grunt work for you another mathy job is finance you can do a masters in financial engineering or mathematical finance and get a quantitative analyst job at a financial services firm']

Name: 96_math_theoretical_succeed_computational
Representative_Docs: ['here s what i ll say based on a papers i ve been reading and researchers i ve been talking to you don t need a particular major insofar as you need a particular set of skills what you need is a deep understanding of calculus of linear algebra of statistics and of probability theory a comfort with proofs and with formal language in order for your thoughts to help anybody else you do need to get someone to publish them and being able to write a proof in typical fashion is helpful a willingness to work with others good communication skills are absolutely essential to get people to agree to work with you everybody wants to be newton whiling away the hours solving math problems on their own but science doesn t work like that anymore only a tiny fraction of the papers i ve seen have fewer than authors and quite a couple have north of obviously domain knowledge of machine learning and ai the specific area you specialize in will have to be to taste there are people who work on fundamental techniques attempting to produce novel optimizers and architectures for models that span across every subfield there are people doing computer vision and reinforcement learning and natural language and so on techniques from each of them are good to know in order to allow for cross breeding but you can t try and know everything about everything eventually you do need to pick a specific problem or two and focus on it basically any subject that gives you the background you need and will let your school agree to have you take upper level ml courses is good cs compe ee math physics quantitative economics statistics and countless others show up in the cvs of top researchers do something that makes you happy', 'to expand on the extremely insightful post already here cough you ll want to go for a degree that focuses heavily on the mathematical theory behind computer science computer science is basically applied mathematics many of the concepts covered on the theoretical side of cs find their basis in discrete mathematics any bs will do any specialization you do at the bachelor level won t really mean anything what will matter is what trajectory your schoolwork sets you on for graduate level education most careers in ai ml are going to require you to have done research in those areas as it shows a strong theoretical basis and that you have the research skills you need to succeed in positions that are building ai you ll need just as many math classes to succeed in the theoretical side of cs as you would need for mechanical engineering your best bet is to take those classes on the side of cs classes to build your knowledge base for cs i d say the core classes to succeed well at the bs level are calc statistics discrete mathematics to succeed at the graduate level calc linear algebra differential equations your research really starts at the graduate level your main focus for now if this is truly the career path you re passionate about should be to find a solid cs program any accredited state school will work and do well in your courses and get involved in undergraduate research make connections with your professors ta s and use them as recommendations for when you apply to graduate programs', 'i have some experience in a few computational fields including a lot in neuroscience and a little fluid dynamics so maybe i can help give you some insight even though these are such different fields from solid mechanics if it helps to know i am trained in applied math i might just end up things you already know and i apologize if that s the case computation is a very broad term but generally you can expect that you wouldn t need a strong math background to succeed instead you can expect to program full time and will need to love programming i work with trivially small datasets in my work and can get away with sloppy programming but computational work often pushes computers to the bleeding edge with large detailed simulations you might find yourself spending a lot of time and energy writing the most cpu and memory efficient program is this something you find interesting computational work often involves implementing ideas from papers that can range from clear and algorithmic to very abstract and theoretical nowadays many people share their code freely so you will often only read and modify code of course being good at thinking mathematically helps but my opinion is that having a phd in applied math for example is overkill if you want to do computation at the same time i don t think you should shy away from more theory math just because you weren t good at it in undergrad it is challenging but in my humble opinion you will be better off confronting your weaknesses everything depends on the program too you should see what the prelim comprehensive exam requirements look like for each school some might be much more mathematical than others even if you are in a more applied program some phd advisors will prefer to do more theoretical research work than computation also they type of computational work can vary a great deal between schools and between professors within schools keep these things in mind if you haven t considered them with computer power improving every year and ai in its relative infancy a lot of the theory is years old but it s gotten much more attention in recent years big data and computation seem like a good choice if you care about looking marketable for either industry or academia however i plan on being a career academic with biological interests so you should consult others in the field if you can if possible email phd students postdocs in the area and pick their brains over coffee']

Representative_Docs: ['so i m a senior studying computer science and at this point while i still have a lot to learn i m fairly good at what i do i work on my own projects whenever i have the time i m good bit of languages though i do need to brush up on sql so i m not trying to advertise dbms services oh yeah and while i m not quite to sys admin level yet i can do just about damn near anything on linux because i spent the last for years obsessively tinkering with it x b so far i set up one job on fiverr offering to debug other students programs but i ran into an issue i m not sure what i should charge what is a reasonable fee given my level of skill and the need of the likely clientele i basically set the price on that to if i found the bug in less than minutes given that most of the people willing to pay for help are likely very new i imagined the bugs would be pretty blatant plus i enjoy hunting bugs so pricing wasn t a big deal but i don t know how much i should charge for things like consistent debugging or tutoring in cs math or building web scrapers or building websites or well anything and other than looking at other people s post which is not a good metric because you only know what they post not what they get i m not sure how to find out how much to charge per job given that most information on people who fill these roles is on salary but not on the amount of jobs they handle in a given year how do i go about finding out what i should charge x b i also don t know where to look for work outside of fiverr and freelancer what places are good places to advertise either online or locally because i imagine some mom pop shops need websites or want to automate aspects of their business x b do you guys have any experience in this area', 'first let s consider fields in which you can do freelancing web development obviously there s no running away from front end side you can reduce amount of it by using tools like materialui bootstrap so you get sufficient styling for many customers or by counting in cost of hiring a front end developer to make layout for you but ultimately you can t really go far without additional languages mobile development python is kinda useless in this regard desktop development same as above machine learning data science you will need to learn sql and a lot of stats math anyway not exactly my field of study however so i can t really discuss details of it so yea you are not getting far with knowing just python gt still being able to find something relevant on freelancing websites here s your problem places like upwork or freelancer com are a lemon market they are not a fair ground for any quality based competition they are more of a place where for every job offer there will be offers coming mostly from bots it s also rigged against you as these platforms require you to pay up to list your offer higher you take a significant pay cut compared to taking a job directly i think it s nowadays etc that being said some tips first if you know languages other than english apply to such jobs vastly lower competition and higher chance a client will get to even see your offer in order for your applications to even be visible for employers high enough you need some reputation from complete assignments a catch so to speak but in practice it means that you will need to do some shitty gigs not nearly worth your time at a beginning in all seriousness one of my first programming jobs was writing an article on how ssh works these platforms can provide a place to start but they are not exactly a sustainable system if you get lucky you will have a somewhat sizeable project and your customer will start returning to you for more in the future last but not the least have a portfolio if you decide to make web applications then have something to show off to clients that they can click on and see your works might even be some learning projects if you want actual cash then i suggest an alternative path first do some research on local companies in your area how do their websites look like anything that looks like it can be automated many places overlook their it infrastructure and so it s very possible to call their owner arrange a meeting and show that you can for instance make their site work properly on mobiles as it s currently broken or maybe they are dealing with platforms like ebay amazon and have to manually enter package numbers every time someone buys a product you could write them a system that automates it complete with integrating it with their chosen courier service that s where you can make real cash small to middle companies that don t need to havein house programmers yet could definitely use some programming and have enough money to go through longer and more complex projects however that is assuming your skills are sufficient to provide tools like this', 'gt so what kind of small task job can i do you should know that yourself if you don t it means you can t really do anything yet in general solo jobs you do using c fall under following categories desktop application development frameworks like qt are highly welcome there if you can for instance build an equivalent of windows calculator then you show some promise if you can build a system that can interact with rest soap apis you can find real jobs embedded devices have you worked with arduino before eg to make a thermometer that would send data via bluetooth to a phone wrote any drivers firmware high performance applications video sound editors web servers d rendering of any kind etc if you have expertise with these kinds then you can ask not for mo but for hour indie game development how s your experience with unreal engine if we expand it to just programming not c limited then there s also mobile possible with c but annoying web same and automation doable but c by default lacks many tools in this regard you can t for instance write a program that will load an excel document and copy it s contents to an sql database that easily whereas in python ruby it would be lines of code long story short clients want to use your programming skills to make money they aren t interested in a newbie that understands language syntax but has never really built anything worthwhile months time is unfortunately insufficient to really learn any field to a practical level gt i m live in rd world country can make month is already good enough for me well if all you want is per month then you don t really need programming skills for it you can make this much with any kind of tedious data entry job depends on your salary expectations really but well hour is doable with these kinds which i do consider to be essentially slave labour then again salaries are relative k year in certain locations in usa are borderline poverty and in eastern europe put you in top population sooo if you want programming jobs with your skills this is going to be significantly harder since you aren t at a level where you can just build something and need supervision as in you would cost money not make any yet gt also i m interested to learn about ai should i keep learning c until i have more in depth knowledge about programming or should i learn python right away you should learn the theory language comes second here s where you can get started this provides a good starting resource that explains many concepts in machine learning and will make you write algorithms used in it manually it uses octave matlab as a language of choice but this is not too important code you write in there are basic loops and matrix multiplications that being said machine learning is essentially a field of applied math while it is true that python is a popular choice for scientists your primary problems will not be due to a language but math and statistics related problems there s a reason why companies looking for people working in this field often only consider master degree graduates and higher there s a lot of theory to learn and you won t get anywhere without university level math i am not saying not to learn it mind you skills you learn are useful in all fields at the very least i can t imagine even one in which solid understanding of statistics and data mining won t be helpful but you approach it from the wrong direction if you think about the language before understanding the problem domain']

silicon valley has to offer on a smaller scale pay is good especially when taking into account col but not quite finance examples include google braintree flexport stripe groupon venmo capital one career growth is pretty good within the company and you can easily move to other offices if you want but limited selection here compared to other tech hubs can definitely limit hopping the consulting firms amp investment banks chicago is a huge management consulting amp banking city and all of these firms are ramping up their digital sectors ai etc pay can be quite good if you rise up the ranks the hours travel definitely aren t for everyone though i think it is definitely easier to get a job here out of school than some of the above fwiw examples include mckinsey bcg jp morgan citi accenture etc startups there are quite a few small companies with really solid dev culture here but i will say they are harder to find they hire a lot based on network and until you build up your network it s hard to say find a list of these online but i know quite a few that have really good culture and pay well everything else other companies that are not software focused but need devs the need for engineers here is pretty big and i m constantly spammed with recruiters trying to get me here generally don t pay quite as well as the above but with chicago s col you won t be struggling even here hope this helps']

Name: 104_ml_deere_john deere_experience
Representative_Docs: ['gt require top notch credentials such as a phd and a extensive research experience the type of jobs where they explicitly require a phd and research experience are typically entirely different breeds to ml ai engineers in fact i would argue that it is an entirely different profession e g an academic research profession these are research scientist jobs not ml engineer jobs they are geared towards people who want to do bona fide research have been published or maybe even have post doc experience basically if you are not qualified to apply to post doc fellowships at universities then most likely you are not qualified for ml research scientist jobs think of ml engineer and research scientists like nurses and doctors at a hospital they often work together and may have some overlapping work here and there but these are not the same professions', 'i don t have much experience in ml but i do work at google obviously it s really hard to get a research scientist gig at google or any big tech company for that matter you mention it sound ing very fancy and elite but the sheen will wear off quickly when your co workers are all at the same position and people outside of work just think you re an asshole elitist sounds like you still have some time to think about it so you should really decide if ml research or even ml in general is actually for you as a swe at google if you do land in a generic backend team you still have a ton of flexibility to do what you want google s investing heavily in ai which means there s so much material in person and self study courses best practice documents office hours etc internally to dive into you could do projects or switch teams with ease to work on more interesting things maybe you could try getting a job first get an idea of what ml ai is like in the industry and what life is like outside of school and then make a decision about whether or not to pursue a phd after a couple of years also fun fact you made this post on jeff dean s th googleversary neat', 'lets wander over to a job posting i saw when searching for another thread john deere embedded software engineer c developer gt as an embedded software engineer for the john deere intelligent solutions group isg urbandale you will develop c for applications utilizing perception software machine learning or advanced algorithms on john deere vehicles in addition you will gt gt work with other deere units to commercialize perception solutions on platforms gt accelerate application opportunities that require a mix of image processing amp machine learning this position will have a focus on c software development for applications and integration of the advanced algorithm into the application software that runs on embedded hardware down in what makes you stand out gt experience applying image processing opencv matlab image processing toolbox applications machine learning caffe or tensorflow gt master s or ph d in an applicable field if you want to do ml you really need to look into an advanced degree this isn t saying you can t do it without the advanced degree but you will be competing with people who have that degree and employers are looking for that degree']
